channel.title: The Strategy Files — Fay Brink | Houston Real Estate Insights
channel.description: Real intelligence for Houston buyers, sellers, and investors — no fluff, just strategy. From Fay Brink, REALTOR® at Renaissance Premier Realty Group, brokered by Blair Realty Group.

item.title: Houston Flood & Insurance: What Every Buyer Needs to Know Before Making an Offer
item.description: A licensed All Lines Claims Adjuster turned REALTOR explains CLUE reports, FEMA zones, flood insurance pitfalls, and why the insurance quote comes before the offer.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Houston School Districts: What Home Buyers Actually Need to Know
item.description: Cy-Fair, Katy ISD, Spring Branch, HISD, Tomball, Klein — a working guide to navigating Houston school districts for home buyers.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Inner Loop vs. Suburbs: The Houston Buyer's Real Decision
item.description: Heights vs Katy, Montrose vs Cypress — the trade-offs, the framework, and why both can be right depending on your life.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: How to Buy New Construction in Houston Without Getting Burned
item.description: Builder incentives, contracts, lot premiums, phase inspections, and what the builder rep won't tell you.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Clarity-Driven Real Estate Decisions with Fay Brink
item.description: Podcast episode: due diligence before offers, flood and insurance risk in Houston, and how a nursing and claims background shapes real estate representation.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Selling Your Home During a Divorce: What No One Tells You
item.description: The financial decisions, emotional pitfalls, mediation strategy, and how to protect your equity during a Texas divorce home sale.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Rent vs. Own: Is Now a Good Time to Buy in the Houston Metro?
item.description: A straight look at the Houston market math, interest rate reality, and when buying still makes sense right now.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: How to Choose a Real Estate Agent in Houston
item.description: What to actually look for — beyond reviews and headshots — when you're picking an agent for one of the biggest decisions of your life.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: How to Write a Strong Offer in a Competitive Houston Market
item.description: Escalation clauses, option periods, earnest money positioning, and what actually moves the needle when sellers have choices.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Why FSBO Sellers Usually Net Less Than They Think
item.description: The data and the reasoning behind why for-sale-by-owner sellers frequently walk away with less than they would have with representation.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Should Your Agent Also Be Your Lender? No.
item.description: The conflict of interest that most buyers don't think about — and why keeping those roles separate protects you.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: How to Actually Compare Loan Estimates
item.description: A practical guide to reading the three-page document that determines what your home really costs over time.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Do I Really Need a Realtor If I Can Search Online?
item.description: What the data and the MLS actually show versus what Zillow and Redfin let you see — and why the gap matters.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: How to Spot Hidden Gems in Online Listings
item.description: The signals that tell an experienced agent a property is worth looking at — and the red flags that don't show up in the photos.
item.author: Fay.Brink@BlairRG.com (Fay Brink)

item.title: Where to Live in Houston When You Work at the Texas Medical Center
item.description: A former Houston nurse explains the real commute picture from every major neighborhood near the TMC — Museum District, Bellaire, West U, Braeswood, Westbury, Midtown, and Montrose.

item.title: Best Neighborhoods for Night Shift Workers in Houston
item.description: Safe at midnight, quiet for day sleeping, close enough to matter. Which Houston neighborhoods actually work for people on overnight healthcare schedules.

item.title: Buying a Home in Houston During Residency: What Residents and Fellows Need to Know
item.description: Physician mortgage programs, the right timeline question, and what price range actually works on a resident salary in one of the country's most affordable major medical markets.
